questions.prompt: What's fundamentally different about shadcn/ui vs traditional component libraries?

choices.text: You don't install components as a dependency — you copy the source code into your repo. `npx shadcn-ui@latest add button` drops `components/ui/button.tsx` into your project. You own the file; you can edit, restyle, delete, or extend it freely with no fork. No version lock, no library updates breaking your code, no theme override gymnastics. You import from `@/components/ui/button` (your code), not `@shadcn/ui`. Built on Radix primitives for accessibility, styled with Tailwind, configured via CSS variables for theming. Default choice for React+Tailwind in 2026.

questions.explanation: Components are files in YOUR repo, not deps. Own, customize, no version lock.

questions.prompt: shadcn ships a new version of Button with a new feature you want. How to update?

choices.text: Three options. (1) Diff manually — compare the new version on the docs site to your modified file; apply only the changes you want. (2) Re-run `npx shadcn-ui@latest add button --overwrite` to take the new version, then port your customizations back. (3) Stay frozen — if your version works, you don't have to update at all; updates are optional, on your schedule. The trade-off vs installable libraries is: more manual update work, but no surprise breaking changes and total control over your component code.

questions.explanation: Manual diff, overwrite-and-port, or stay frozen. Updates are optional and you control timing.
